Microbiome-TNC

  • 1 Import data to qiime2
  • 2 Demultiplex fastq files in qiime2
  • 3 Export data to analyze in R
  • 4 Inspect read quality profiles
  • 5 Place filtered files in filtered/ subdirectory
  • 6 Learn the Error Rates
  • 7 Merge paired reads, Non-overlapping reads
  • 8 Construct sequence table
  • 9 Remove chimeras
  • 10 Track reads through the pipeline
  • 11 Assign taxonomy
  • 12 Phyloseq
  • 13 Making a phyloseq object
  • 14 Alpha Diversity, Time
  • 15 Making a PCoA plot
  • 16 Making a Phylum taxa plot
  • 17 Making a Class taxa plot
  • 18 Making a Order taxa plot
  • 19 Making a Family taxa plot
  • 20 Making a Genus taxa plot

1 Import data to qiime2

# # Creat a folder name emp-paired-end-sequence to store these 3 files:
# # Rename _R1.fastq.gz to forward.fastq.gz
# # Rename _R2.fastq.gz to reverse.fastq.gz
# # Rename _I1.fastq.gz to barcode.fastq.gz
# #----------------------------------------
# # Type the following command lines in terminal
# qiime tools import \
# --type EMPPairedEndSequences \
# --input-path emp-paired-end-sequences \
# --output-path emp-paired-end-sequences.qza

2 Demultiplex fastq files in qiime2

# # metafile.txt is your text file which summarizes the data, sample, method.
# # BarcodeSequence is the column that has barcode in the meta file.
# # These things need to be modified based on your data
# qiime demux emp-paired \
# --i-seqs emp-paired-end-sequences.qza \
# --m-barcodes-file metafile.txt \
# --m-barcode-column BarcodeSequence \
# --p-rev-comp-mapping-barcodes \
# --o-per-sample-sequences demux.qza \
# --o-error-correction-details demux-details.qza

3 Export data to analyze in R

# qiime tools export \
#   --input-path demux.qza \
#   --output-path exported-demux
library(dada2)
## Loading required package: Rcpp
path <- "~/Microbiome/XinLuo_Haibo_2022_data/exported_demux" 
head(list.files(path))
## [1] "1_59_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz"   "1_59_L001_R2_001.fastq.gz"  
## [3] "10_10_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz"  "10_10_L001_R2_001.fastq.gz" 
## [5] "100_46_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz" "100_46_L001_R2_001.fastq.gz"
# Forward and reverse fastq filenames have format
fnFs <- sort(list.files(path, pattern="_R1_001.fastq", full.names = TRUE))
fnRs <- sort(list.files(path, pattern="_R2_001.fastq", full.names = TRUE))

# Extract sample names, assuming filenames have format: SAMPLENAME_XXX.fastq
sample.names <- sapply(strsplit(basename(fnFs), "_"), `[`, 1)

4 Inspect read quality profiles

plotQualityProfile(fnFs[70:71])
## Warning: `guides(<scale> = FALSE)` is deprecated. Please use `guides(<scale> =
## "none")` instead.

plotQualityProfile(fnRs[50:52])
## Warning: `guides(<scale> = FALSE)` is deprecated. Please use `guides(<scale> =
## "none")` instead.

5 Place filtered files in filtered/ subdirectory

filtFs <- file.path(path, "filtered", paste0(sample.names, "_F_filt.fastq.gz"))
filtRs <- file.path(path, "filtered", paste0(sample.names, "_R_filt.fastq.gz"))
names(filtFs) <- sample.names
names(filtRs) <- sample.names

#Use the plot error to find truncLen and trimLeft
out <- filterAndTrim(fnFs, filtFs, fnRs, filtRs, truncLen=c(50,100),trimLeft = c(0,3),
              maxN=0, maxEE=c(2,2), truncQ=0, rm.phix=TRUE,
              compress=TRUE, multithread=TRUE) 
## Creating output directory: /home/tnchau/Microbiome/XinLuo_Haibo_2022_data/exported_demux/filtered
head(out)
##                             reads.in reads.out
## 1_59_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz        254       201
## 10_10_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz      4089      3266
## 100_46_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz       79        61
## 11_91_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz       213       152
## 12_7_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz       1802      1439
## 13_16_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz      1549      1135

6 Learn the Error Rates

errF <- learnErrors(filtFs, multithread=TRUE)
## 3216250 total bases in 64325 reads from 100 samples will be used for learning the error rates.
errR <- learnErrors(filtRs, multithread=TRUE)
## 6239525 total bases in 64325 reads from 100 samples will be used for learning the error rates.
plotErrors(errF, nominalQ=TRUE)
## Warning: Transformation introduced infinite values in continuous y-axis

## Warning: Transformation introduced infinite values in continuous y-axis

# Sample Inference

dadaFs <- dada(filtFs, err=errF, multithread=TRUE)
## Sample 1 - 201 reads in 109 unique sequences.
## Sample 2 - 3266 reads in 851 unique sequences.

dadaRs <- dada(filtRs, err=errR, multithread=TRUE)

7 Merge paired reads, Non-overlapping reads

mergers <- mergePairs(dadaFs, filtFs, dadaRs, filtRs, justConcatenate=TRUE)

8 Construct sequence table

seqtab <- makeSequenceTable(mergers)
dim(seqtab)
## [1]  100 1195
 # Inspect distribution of sequence lengths
table(nchar(getSequences(seqtab)))
## 
##  157 
## 1195

9 Remove chimeras

seqtab.nochim <- removeBimeraDenovo(seqtab, method="consensus", multithread=TRUE, verbose=TRUE)
## Identified 999 bimeras out of 1195 input sequences.
dim(seqtab.nochim)
## [1] 100 196
sum(seqtab.nochim)/sum(seqtab)
## [1] 0.8783641

10 Track reads through the pipeline

getN <- function(x) sum(getUniques(x))
track <- cbind(out, sapply(dadaFs, getN), sapply(dadaRs, getN), sapply(mergers, getN), rowSums(seqtab.nochim))
# If processing a single sample, remove the sapply calls: e.g. replace sapply(dadaFs, getN) with getN(dadaFs)
colnames(track) <- c("input", "filtered", "denoisedF", "denoisedR", "merged", "nonchim")
rownames(track) <- sample.names
head(track)
##     input filtered denoisedF denoisedR merged nonchim
## 1     254      201       201       198    198     190
## 10   4089     3266      3263      3210   3208    2748
## 100    79       61        61        59     59      53
## 11    213      152       145       142    139     103
## 12   1802     1439      1423      1422   1408    1249
## 13   1549     1135      1120      1114   1102     986

11 Assign taxonomy

taxa <- assignTaxonomy(seqtab.nochim, "~/Microbiome/XinLuo_Haibo_2022_data/exported_demux/silva_nr99_v138.1_train_set.fa.gz", multithread=TRUE)
taxa[is.na(taxa)] <- "NA"

#taxa <- addSpecies(taxa, "~/Microbiome/XinLuo_Haibo_2022_data/exported_demux/silva_species_assignment_v138.1.fa.gz")
taxa.print <- taxa # Removing sequence rownames for display only
rownames(taxa.print) <- NULL
head(taxa.print)
##      Kingdom    Phylum           Class                
## [1,] "Bacteria" "Bacteroidota"   "Bacteroidia"        
## [2,] "Bacteria" "Firmicutes"     "Clostridia"         
## [3,] "Bacteria" "Proteobacteria" "Gammaproteobacteria"
## [4,] "Bacteria" "Bacteroidota"   "Bacteroidia"        
## [5,] "Bacteria" "Firmicutes"     "Negativicutes"      
## [6,] "Bacteria" "Bacteroidota"   "Bacteroidia"        
##      Order                            Family              
## [1,] "Bacteroidales"                  "Prevotellaceae"    
## [2,] "Clostridiales"                  "Clostridiaceae"    
## [3,] "Enterobacterales"               "Enterobacteriaceae"
## [4,] "Bacteroidales"                  "Prevotellaceae"    
## [5,] "Veillonellales-Selenomonadales" "Veillonellaceae"   
## [6,] "Bacteroidales"                  "Prevotellaceae"    
##      Genus                        
## [1,] "Prevotella_9"               
## [2,] "Clostridium sensu stricto 1"
## [3,] "NA"                         
## [4,] "Prevotella_7"               
## [5,] "Megasphaera"                
## [6,] "Prevotellaceae NK3B31 group"

12 Phyloseq

library(phyloseq)
library(Biostrings)
## Loading required package: BiocGenerics
## Loading required package: parallel
## 
## Attaching package: 'BiocGenerics'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:parallel':
## 
##     clusterApply, clusterApplyLB, clusterCall, clusterEvalQ,
##     clusterExport, clusterMap, parApply, parCapply, parLapply,
##     parLapplyLB, parRapply, parSapply, parSapplyLB
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     IQR, mad, sd, var, xtabs
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     anyDuplicated, append, as.data.frame, basename, cbind, colnames,
##     dirname, do.call, duplicated, eval, evalq, Filter, Find, get, grep,
##     grepl, intersect, is.unsorted, lapply, Map, mapply, match, mget,
##     order, paste, pmax, pmax.int, pmin, pmin.int, Position, rank,
##     rbind, Reduce, rownames, sapply, setdiff, sort, table, tapply,
##     union, unique, unsplit, which.max, which.min
## Loading required package: S4Vectors
## Loading required package: stats4
## 
## Attaching package: 'S4Vectors'
## The following object is masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     expand.grid
## Loading required package: IRanges
## 
## Attaching package: 'IRanges'
## The following object is masked from 'package:phyloseq':
## 
##     distance
## Loading required package: XVector
## 
## Attaching package: 'Biostrings'
## The following object is masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     strsplit
#library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching packages ─────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse 1.3.0 ──
## ✓ ggplot2 3.3.5     ✓ purrr   0.3.4
## ✓ tibble  3.0.6     ✓ dplyr   1.0.4
## ✓ tidyr   1.1.2     ✓ stringr 1.4.0
## ✓ readr   1.4.0     ✓ forcats 0.5.1
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## x dplyr::collapse()   masks Biostrings::collapse(), IRanges::collapse()
## x dplyr::combine()    masks BiocGenerics::combine()
## x purrr::compact()    masks XVector::compact()
## x dplyr::desc()       masks IRanges::desc()
## x tidyr::expand()     masks S4Vectors::expand()
## x dplyr::filter()     masks stats::filter()
## x dplyr::first()      masks S4Vectors::first()
## x dplyr::lag()        masks stats::lag()
## x ggplot2::Position() masks BiocGenerics::Position(), base::Position()
## x purrr::reduce()     masks IRanges::reduce()
## x dplyr::rename()     masks S4Vectors::rename()
## x dplyr::slice()      masks XVector::slice(), IRanges::slice()
library(dplyr)
theme_set(theme_bw())

sdata2 = read.table(file = "~/Microbiome/XinLuo_Haibo_2022_data/211103_Lou_16sFWD_211029_secondVersion.txt", header = TRUE)
sdata2$SampleID = as.character(sdata2$SampleID)
head(sdata2)
##   SampleID BarcodeSequence  LinkerPrimerSequence BarcodePlate Well Description
## 1        1    TCAGCCTCAGCC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14   A1           1
## 2        2    TGCAGGGAACCG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14   A2           2
## 3        3    CAGCGCGGCTAG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14   A3           3
## 4        4    ATGGACCTAGCT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14   A4           4
## 5        5    CTAGCCCGTTCG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14   A5           5
## 6        6    ACGGGCACGACA GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14   A6           6
##   Time     Method Add_num       sample    method_time
## 1  12h      blank       1      blank-1      blank-12h
## 2  12h      blank       2      blank-2      blank-12h
## 3  12h      blank       3      blank-3      blank-12h
## 4  12h Corn-fiber       1 Corn-fiber-1 Corn-fiber-12h
## 5  12h Corn-fiber       2 Corn-fiber-2 Corn-fiber-12h
## 6  12h Corn-fiber       3 Corn-fiber-3 Corn-fiber-12h
# sdata3 <- sdata2 %>%
#   column_to_rownames(var = "SampleID")
# sdata3
# 
# #-------------Load the sequence table
# sequence_table = seqtab.nochim
# #1. Remove the column names-----
# colnames(sequence_table) <- NULL 
# #2. Change format to data frame----------------
# sequence_table <- as.data.frame(sequence_table) 
# #3. Add header to “SampleID” column-----------------------------------
# sequence_table <- rownames_to_column(sequence_table, var = "SampleID") 
# #4. Add metadata to sequence table----------
# seq_table <- sdata2 %>%  
#   left_join(sequence_table, by = "SampleID")
# seq_table
# 
# #5. Remove metadata columns from the sequence table and change the “SampleID” column to row names-------------------------------
# subset_all <- seq_table %>%
#   column_to_rownames(var = "SampleID")%>%
#   select(-c(BarcodeSequence, LinkerPrimerSequence, BarcodePlate, Well, Description, Time, Method, Add_num, sample, method_time))
# 
# colnames(subset_all) <- NULL # Remove column names from sequence table
# subset_all
# 
# #---------------Load data for taxa table:

13 Making a phyloseq object

ps <- phyloseq(otu_table(seqtab.nochim, taxa_are_rows=FALSE), 
               sample_data(sdata2[order(sdata2$SampleID),]), 
               tax_table(taxa))
ps
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 196 taxa and 100 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 100 samples by 11 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 196 taxa by 6 taxonomic ranks ]
# Clean phyloseq --------------------------------------------------------------------------
samplesover100_all <- subset_samples(ps, sample_sums(ps) > 100)
prune_samplesover100_all <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(samplesover100_all) > 0, samplesover100_all)
set.seed(81)
rarefy_samplesover100_all <- rarefy_even_depth(prune_samplesover100_all, rngseed= 81, sample.size = min(sample_sums(prune_samplesover100_all)))
## `set.seed(81)` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.
## Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.
## Try `set.seed(81); .Random.seed` for the full vector
## ...
## 55OTUs were removed because they are no longer 
## present in any sample after random subsampling
## ...
bbps <- rarefy_samplesover100_all


#---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sample_colors <- c("blank"  = "lightgoldenrod", "Corn-fiber" = "darkorange", 
                   "Wet-milling-corn-fiber" = "maroon", "DDR-NaOH" = "indianred2",
                   "DDR-NaOH-ball" = "red3", "DDR-NaHCO3"  = "tan4", 
                   "DDR-NaHCO3-ball"  = "lightblue", "Inulin" = "green", "Unknown"  ="gray")
sample_types <- unique(sdata2$Method)
sample_labels <- unique(sdata2$Method)
#---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

bbps %>%                                                              #phyloseq object
  plot_richness(
    x = "Method",                                                #compare diversity of datatype
    measures = c("Observed", "Shannon")) +                           #choose diversity measures
  geom_violin(aes(fill = Method), show.legend = FALSE)+          #make violin plot, set fill aes to sampletype
  geom_boxplot(width=0.1) +                                          #add boxplot, set width
  theme_classic()+                                                   #change theme to classic
  xlab(NULL)+                                                        #no label on x-axis
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 20),                  #adjust y-axis text
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, angle = 90),           #adjust x-axis label position
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20))+                     #adjust y-axis title
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 25))+        #adjust headings
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_colors)+   #set fill colors
  scale_x_discrete(                                                  #change x-axis labels
    breaks = sample_types, 
    labels = sample_labels)+                   
  ggtitle("Alpha Diversity") +                                       #add title
  theme(plot.title=element_text(size = 25, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) #change title size, face and position

14 Alpha Diversity, Time

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
sample_colors <- c("12h"  = "lightgoldenrod", "24h" = "darkorange", "48h" = "maroon", 
                    "72h"  = "lightblue", "0h"  = "green")
sample_types <- unique(sdata2$Time)
sample_labels <- unique(sdata2$Time)
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

bbps %>%                                                              #phyloseq object
  plot_richness(
    x = "Time",                                                #compare diversity of datatype
    measures = c("Observed", "Shannon")) +                           #choose diversity measures
  geom_violin(aes(fill = Time), show.legend = FALSE)+          #make violin plot, set fill aes to sampletype
  geom_boxplot(width=0.1) +                                          #add boxplot, set width
  theme_classic()+                                                   #change theme to classic
  xlab(NULL)+                                                        #no label on x-axis
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 20),                  #adjust y-axis text
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, angle = 90),           #adjust x-axis label position
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20))+                     #adjust y-axis title
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 25))+        #adjust headings
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_colors)+   #set fill colors
  scale_x_discrete(                                                  #change x-axis labels
    breaks = sample_types, 
    labels = sample_labels)+                   
  ggtitle("Alpha Diversity") +                                       #add title
  theme(plot.title=element_text(size = 25, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) #change title size, face and position

15 Making a PCoA plot

all_pcoa <- ordinate(
  physeq = bbps, 
  method = "PCoA", 
  distance = "bray"
)
sample_colors <- c("blank"  = "lightgoldenrod", "Corn-fiber" = "darkorange", 
                   "Wet-milling-corn-fiber" = "maroon", "DDR-NaOH" = "indianred2",
                   "DDR-NaOH-ball" = "red3", "DDR-NaHCO3"  = "tan4", 
                   "DDR-NaHCO3-ball"  = "lightblue", "Inulin" = "green", "Unknown"  ="gray")

plot_ordination(
  physeq = bbps,                                                         #phyloseq object
  ordination = all_pcoa)+                                                #ordination
  geom_point(aes(fill = Method, shape = Time), size = 3) +     #sets fill color to sampletype
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(21, 22, 23,24,25))+
  scale_fill_manual(values = sample_colors) +
  theme_classic() +                                                      #changes theme, removes grey background
  theme(                             
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),                               #changes legend size
    legend.title = element_blank(),                                      #removes legend title
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black"))+  #adds black boarder around legend
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size = 20),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20))+
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 21)))           #fills legend points based on the fill command

16 Making a Phylum taxa plot

# Filter out eukaryotes and mitochondria
justbacteria <- bbps %>%
  subset_taxa(
    Kingdom == "Bacteria" &                   #only bacteria
      Family  != "mitochondria" &             #filter out mitochondria
      Class   != "Chloroplast"                #filter out chloroplasts
  )
justbacteria
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 138 taxa and 75 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 75 samples by 11 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 138 taxa by 6 taxonomic ranks ]
# Convert phyloseq object into a data frame with relative abundance counts
phylumabundance <- justbacteria %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>%                     # agglomerate at phylum level
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)} ) %>% # Transform to rel. abundance
  psmelt() %>%                                         # Melt to long format
  arrange(Phylum) 
head(phylumabundance)
##                                                                                                                                                             OTU
## 1 TACGGAAGGTCCGGGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCTTAGATATCACGAAGAACTCCGATTGCGAAGGCAGCTCACTGGAGCGCAACTGACGCTGAAGCTCGAAAGTGCGGGTATCGAAC
## 2 TACGGAAGGTCCGGGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCTTAGATATCACGAAGAACTCCGATTGCGAAGGCAGCTCACTGGAGCGCAACTGACGCTGAAGCTCGAAAGTGCGGGTATCGAAC
## 3 TACGGAAGGTCCGGGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCTTAGATATCACGAAGAACTCCGATTGCGAAGGCAGCTCACTGGAGCGCAACTGACGCTGAAGCTCGAAAGTGCGGGTATCGAAC
## 4 TACGGAAGGTCCGGGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCTTAGATATCACGAAGAACTCCGATTGCGAAGGCAGCTCACTGGAGCGCAACTGACGCTGAAGCTCGAAAGTGCGGGTATCGAAC
## 5 TACGGAAGGTCCGGGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCTTAGATATCACGAAGAACTCCGATTGCGAAGGCAGCTCACTGGAGCGCAACTGACGCTGAAGCTCGAAAGTGCGGGTATCGAAC
## 6 TACGGAAGGTCCGGGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCTTAGATATCACGAAGAACTCCGATTGCGAAGGCAGCTCACTGGAGCGCAACTGACGCTGAAGCTCGAAAGTGCGGGTATCGAAC
##   Sample Abundance SampleID BarcodeSequence  LinkerPrimerSequence BarcodePlate
## 1      9 0.8737864        9    ACCCAGTATGGT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 2     38 0.8058252       38    TAGCGGAAGACG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 3     96 0.7864078       96    GCTATCAAGACA GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 4     49 0.7281553       49    GTAACCACCACC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 5     45 0.7281553       45    GCGTCGGTACGG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 6     32 0.7087379       32    TGTACCAACCGA GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
##   Well Description Time                 Method Add_num                   sample
## 1   A9           9  12h Wet-milling-corn-fiber       3 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-3
## 2   D2          38  24h          DDR-NaOH-ball       2          DDR-NaOH-ball-2
## 3  H12          96  72h                 Inulin       3                 Inulin-3
## 4   E1          49  48h                  blank       1                  blank-1
## 5   D9          45  24h        DDR-NaHCO3-ball       3        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-3
## 6   C8          32  24h Wet-milling-corn-fiber       2 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-2
##                  method_time  Kingdom       Phylum
## 1 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-12h Bacteria Bacteroidota
## 2          DDR-NaOH-ball-24h Bacteria Bacteroidota
## 3                 Inulin-72h Bacteria Bacteroidota
## 4                  blank-48h Bacteria Bacteroidota
## 5        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-24h Bacteria Bacteroidota
## 6 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-24h Bacteria Bacteroidota
# Summarize the most abundant taxa, filter to only include taxa with > 1% abundance
all <- phylumabundance %>%
  select(Phylum, Method, Abundance, Time) %>%
  group_by(Phylum, Method, Time) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_abundance = mean(Abundance)
  ) %>%
  filter(avg_abundance > 0.1)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Phylum', 'Method'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
all
## # A tibble: 92 x 4
## # Groups:   Phylum, Method [26]
##    Phylum       Method     Time  avg_abundance
##    <chr>        <chr>      <chr>         <dbl>
##  1 Bacteroidota blank      12h           0.417
##  2 Bacteroidota blank      24h           0.204
##  3 Bacteroidota blank      48h           0.382
##  4 Bacteroidota blank      72h           0.393
##  5 Bacteroidota Corn-fiber 12h           0.463
##  6 Bacteroidota Corn-fiber 24h           0.485
##  7 Bacteroidota Corn-fiber 48h           0.359
##  8 Bacteroidota Corn-fiber 72h           0.534
##  9 Bacteroidota DDR-NaHCO3 24h           0.612
## 10 Bacteroidota DDR-NaHCO3 48h           0.372
## # … with 82 more rows
phylum_colors <- c("lightgoldenrod", "darkorange", "maroon", "indianred2", 
                    "red3", "tan4",   "lightblue",  "green","gray")

ggplot(all)+
  geom_col(mapping = aes(x = Method, y = avg_abundance, fill = Phylum), position = "fill", show.legend = TRUE)+
  ylab("Proportion of Community") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_colors) +
  xlab(NULL)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        title = element_text(size = 15))

17 Making a Class taxa plot

# Convert phyloseq object into a data frame with relative abundance counts
classabundance <- justbacteria %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Class") %>%                     # agglomerate at phylum level
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)} ) %>% # Transform to rel. abundance
  psmelt() %>%                                         # Melt to long format
  arrange(Class) 
head(classabundance)
##                                                                                                                                                             OTU
## 1 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGAGGGAGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGACGGTCTGGTCCGCAACTGACGCTCAGTCCCGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 2 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGAGGGAGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGACGGTCTGGTCCGCAACTGACGCTCAGTCCCGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 3 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGAGGGAGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGACGGTCTGGTCCGCAACTGACGCTCAGTCCCGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 4 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGAGGGAGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGACGGTCTGGTCCGCAACTGACGCTCAGTCCCGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 5 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGAGGGAGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGACGGTCTGGTCCGCAACTGACGCTCAGTCCCGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 6 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGAGGGAGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGACGGTCTGGTCCGCAACTGACGCTCAGTCCCGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
##   Sample  Abundance SampleID BarcodeSequence  LinkerPrimerSequence BarcodePlate
## 1     24 0.32038835       24    CTGACGATCCGT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 2     99 0.20388350       99    CATACCGTGAGT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           15
## 3     85 0.16504854       85    ATCTAAGTTACT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 4     49 0.15533981       49    GTAACCACCACC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 5     51 0.11650485       51    TGAAGTCACAGT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 6     35 0.09708738       35    TGCCATTAGAGC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
##   Well Description Time        Method Add_num          sample       method_time
## 1  B12          24  12h        Inulin       3        Inulin-3        Inulin-12h
## 2   A3          99   0h       Unknown       0       Unknown-0        Unknown-0h
## 3   H1          85  72h DDR-NaOH-ball       1 DDR-NaOH-ball-1 DDR-NaOH-ball-72h
## 4   E1          49  48h         blank       1         blank-1         blank-48h
## 5   E3          51  48h         blank       3         blank-3         blank-48h
## 6  C11          35  24h      DDR-NaOH       2      DDR-NaOH-2      DDR-NaOH-24h
##    Kingdom     Phylum   Class
## 1 Bacteria Firmicutes Bacilli
## 2 Bacteria Firmicutes Bacilli
## 3 Bacteria Firmicutes Bacilli
## 4 Bacteria Firmicutes Bacilli
## 5 Bacteria Firmicutes Bacilli
## 6 Bacteria Firmicutes Bacilli
# Summarize the most abundant taxa, filter to only include taxa with > 1% abundance
all <- classabundance %>%
  select(Class, Method, Abundance, Time) %>%
  group_by(Class, Method, Time) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_abundance = mean(Abundance)
  ) %>%
  filter(avg_abundance > 0.1)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Class', 'Method'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
all
## # A tibble: 94 x 4
## # Groups:   Class, Method [29]
##    Class       Method     Time  avg_abundance
##    <chr>       <chr>      <chr>         <dbl>
##  1 Bacilli     Inulin     12h           0.320
##  2 Bacteroidia blank      12h           0.417
##  3 Bacteroidia blank      24h           0.204
##  4 Bacteroidia blank      48h           0.382
##  5 Bacteroidia blank      72h           0.393
##  6 Bacteroidia Corn-fiber 12h           0.463
##  7 Bacteroidia Corn-fiber 24h           0.485
##  8 Bacteroidia Corn-fiber 48h           0.359
##  9 Bacteroidia Corn-fiber 72h           0.534
## 10 Bacteroidia DDR-NaHCO3 24h           0.612
## # … with 84 more rows
class_colors <- c("lightgoldenrod", "darkorange", "maroon", "indianred2", 
                    "red3", "tan4",   "lightblue",  "green","gray")

ggplot(all)+
  geom_col(mapping = aes(x = Method, y = avg_abundance, fill = Class), position = "fill", show.legend = TRUE)+
  ylab("Proportion of Community") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = class_colors) +
  xlab(NULL)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        title = element_text(size = 15))

18 Making a Order taxa plot

# Convert phyloseq object into a data frame with relative abundance counts
orderabundance <- justbacteria %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Order") %>%                     # agglomerate at phylum level
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)} ) %>% # Transform to rel. abundance
  psmelt() %>%                                         # Melt to long format
  arrange(Order) 
head(orderabundance)
##                                                                                                                                                             OTU
## 1 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTGTCCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGAGCATGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTGGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGCCTTTCTGGACTGTGTCTGACGCTGAGATGCGAAAGCCAGGGTAGCAAAC
## 2 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTGTCCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGAGCATGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTGGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGCCTTTCTGGACTGTGTCTGACGCTGAGATGCGAAAGCCAGGGTAGCAAAC
## 3 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTGTCCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGAGCATGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTGGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGCCTTTCTGGACTGTGTCTGACGCTGAGATGCGAAAGCCAGGGTAGCAAAC
## 4 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTGTCCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGAGCATGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTGGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGCCTTTCTGGACTGTGTCTGACGCTGAGATGCGAAAGCCAGGGTAGCAAAC
## 5 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTGTCCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGAGCATGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTGGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGCCTTTCTGGACTGTGTCTGACGCTGAGATGCGAAAGCCAGGGTAGCAAAC
## 6 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTGTCCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGAGCATGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTGGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGCCTTTCTGGACTGTGTCTGACGCTGAGATGCGAAAGCCAGGGTAGCAAAC
##   Sample  Abundance SampleID BarcodeSequence  LinkerPrimerSequence BarcodePlate
## 1     71 0.13592233       71    CATGGGTGTTAC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 2     74 0.07766990       74    TCCTGAACACAG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 3      4 0.07766990        4    ATGGACCTAGCT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 4      7 0.07766990        7    TACGAGCCCTAA GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 5     83 0.07766990       83    AATCAACTAGGC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 6     20 0.06796117       20    TCCGTATGCGCC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
##   Well Description Time                 Method Add_num                   sample
## 1  F11          71  48h                 Inulin       2                 Inulin-2
## 2   G2          74  72h                  blank       2                  blank-2
## 3   A4           4  12h             Corn-fiber       1             Corn-fiber-1
## 4   A7           7  12h Wet-milling-corn-fiber       1 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-1
## 5  G11          83  72h               DDR-NaOH       2               DDR-NaOH-2
## 6   B8          20  12h        DDR-NaHCO3-ball       2        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-2
##                  method_time  Kingdom     Phylum         Class
## 1                 Inulin-48h Bacteria Firmicutes Negativicutes
## 2                  blank-72h Bacteria Firmicutes Negativicutes
## 3             Corn-fiber-12h Bacteria Firmicutes Negativicutes
## 4 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-12h Bacteria Firmicutes Negativicutes
## 5               DDR-NaOH-72h Bacteria Firmicutes Negativicutes
## 6        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-12h Bacteria Firmicutes Negativicutes
##               Order
## 1 Acidaminococcales
## 2 Acidaminococcales
## 3 Acidaminococcales
## 4 Acidaminococcales
## 5 Acidaminococcales
## 6 Acidaminococcales
# Summarize the most abundant taxa, filter to only include taxa with > 1% abundance
all <- orderabundance %>%
  select(Order, Method, Abundance, Time) %>%
  group_by(Order, Method, Time) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_abundance = mean(Abundance)
  ) %>%
  filter(avg_abundance > 0.1)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Order', 'Method'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
all
## # A tibble: 91 x 4
## # Groups:   Order, Method [29]
##    Order         Method     Time  avg_abundance
##    <chr>         <chr>      <chr>         <dbl>
##  1 Bacteroidales blank      12h           0.417
##  2 Bacteroidales blank      24h           0.204
##  3 Bacteroidales blank      48h           0.382
##  4 Bacteroidales blank      72h           0.393
##  5 Bacteroidales Corn-fiber 12h           0.463
##  6 Bacteroidales Corn-fiber 24h           0.485
##  7 Bacteroidales Corn-fiber 48h           0.359
##  8 Bacteroidales Corn-fiber 72h           0.534
##  9 Bacteroidales DDR-NaHCO3 24h           0.612
## 10 Bacteroidales DDR-NaHCO3 48h           0.372
## # … with 81 more rows
order_colors <- c("maroon", "indianred2", "lightblue",  "green","gray",
                  "lightgoldenrod", "darkorange")

ggplot(all)+
  geom_col(mapping = aes(x = Method, y = avg_abundance, fill = Order), position = "fill", show.legend = TRUE)+
  ylab("Proportion of Community") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = order_colors) +
  xlab(NULL)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        title = element_text(size = 15))

19 Making a Family taxa plot

# Convert phyloseq object into a data frame with relative abundance counts
familyabundance <- justbacteria %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Family") %>%                     # agglomerate at phylum level
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)} ) %>% # Transform to rel. abundance
  psmelt() %>%                                         # Melt to long format
  arrange(Family) 
head(familyabundance)
##                                                                                                                                                             OTU
## 1 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGAATTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTCGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTCTAACTGACGCTGAGGCTCGAAAGTGTGGGGAGCAAAC
## 2 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGAATTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTCGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTCTAACTGACGCTGAGGCTCGAAAGTGTGGGGAGCAAAC
## 3 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGAATTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTCGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTCTAACTGACGCTGAGGCTCGAAAGTGTGGGGAGCAAAC
## 4 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGAATTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTCGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTCTAACTGACGCTGAGGCTCGAAAGTGTGGGGAGCAAAC
## 5 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGAATTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTCGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTCTAACTGACGCTGAGGCTCGAAAGTGTGGGGAGCAAAC
## 6 TACGTAGGTGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGAATTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGCANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTGAAATGCGTAGATATTCGGAGGAACACCAGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTCTAACTGACGCTGAGGCTCGAAAGTGTGGGGAGCAAAC
##   Sample  Abundance SampleID BarcodeSequence  LinkerPrimerSequence BarcodePlate
## 1     98 0.06930693       98    CAACACATGCTG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           15
## 2     76 0.00000000       76    ACCTAGCTAGTG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 3     18 0.00000000       18    GTATTTCGGACG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 4     77 0.00000000       77    CCAGTTCCAAAG GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 5     61 0.00000000       61    TCTATTGTTAGA GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
## 6     75 0.00000000       75    TAAACGCGACTC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA           14
##   Well Description Time        Method Add_num          sample       method_time
## 1   A2          98   0h       Unknown       0       Unknown-0        Unknown-0h
## 2   G4          76  72h    Corn-fiber       1    Corn-fiber-1    Corn-fiber-72h
## 3   B6          18  12h    DDR-NaHCO3       3    DDR-NaHCO3-3    DDR-NaHCO3-12h
## 4   G5          77  72h    Corn-fiber       2    Corn-fiber-2    Corn-fiber-72h
## 5   F1          61  48h DDR-NaOH-ball       1 DDR-NaOH-ball-1 DDR-NaOH-ball-48h
## 6   G3          75  72h         blank       3         blank-3         blank-72h
##    Kingdom     Phylum      Class           Order
## 1 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia Oscillospirales
## 2 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia Oscillospirales
## 3 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia Oscillospirales
## 4 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia Oscillospirales
## 5 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia Oscillospirales
## 6 Bacteria Firmicutes Clostridia Oscillospirales
##                                  Family
## 1 [Eubacterium] coprostanoligenes group
## 2 [Eubacterium] coprostanoligenes group
## 3 [Eubacterium] coprostanoligenes group
## 4 [Eubacterium] coprostanoligenes group
## 5 [Eubacterium] coprostanoligenes group
## 6 [Eubacterium] coprostanoligenes group
unique(familyabundance$Family)
##  [1] "[Eubacterium] coprostanoligenes group"
##  [2] "Acidaminococcaceae"                   
##  [3] "Anaerovoracaceae"                     
##  [4] "Burkholderiaceae"                     
##  [5] "Butyricicoccaceae"                    
##  [6] "Christensenellaceae"                  
##  [7] "Clostridiaceae"                       
##  [8] "Desulfovibrionaceae"                  
##  [9] "Enterobacteriaceae"                   
## [10] "Erysipelatoclostridiaceae"            
## [11] "Erysipelotrichaceae"                  
## [12] "Fusobacteriaceae"                     
## [13] "Lachnospiraceae"                      
## [14] "Lactobacillaceae"                     
## [15] "Muribaculaceae"                       
## [16] "NA"                                   
## [17] "Oscillospiraceae"                     
## [18] "p-251-o5"                             
## [19] "p-2534-18B5 gut group"                
## [20] "Prevotellaceae"                       
## [21] "Rikenellaceae"                        
## [22] "Ruminococcaceae"                      
## [23] "Selenomonadaceae"                     
## [24] "Spirochaetaceae"                      
## [25] "Streptococcaceae"                     
## [26] "Succinivibrionaceae"                  
## [27] "Sutterellaceae"                       
## [28] "Synergistaceae"                       
## [29] "Tannerellaceae"                       
## [30] "Veillonellaceae"
# Summarize the most abundant taxa, filter to only include taxa with > 1% abundance
all <- familyabundance %>%
  select(Family, Method, Abundance, Time) %>%
  group_by(Family, Method, Time) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_abundance = mean(Abundance)
  ) %>%
  filter(avg_abundance > 0.1)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Family', 'Method'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
all
## # A tibble: 90 x 4
## # Groups:   Family, Method [28]
##    Family         Method     Time  avg_abundance
##    <chr>          <chr>      <chr>         <dbl>
##  1 Clostridiaceae blank      12h           0.343
##  2 Clostridiaceae blank      24h           0.505
##  3 Clostridiaceae blank      48h           0.168
##  4 Clostridiaceae blank      72h           0.112
##  5 Clostridiaceae Corn-fiber 12h           0.168
##  6 Clostridiaceae Corn-fiber 24h           0.228
##  7 Clostridiaceae Corn-fiber 48h           0.286
##  8 Clostridiaceae Corn-fiber 72h           0.262
##  9 Clostridiaceae DDR-NaHCO3 12h           0.524
## 10 Clostridiaceae DDR-NaHCO3 24h           0.165
## # … with 80 more rows
family_colors <-c("maroon", "indianred2", "lightblue",  "green","gray",
                  "lightgoldenrod", "darkorange")

ggplot(all)+
  geom_col(mapping = aes(x = Method, y = avg_abundance, fill = Family), position = "fill", show.legend = TRUE)+
  ylab("Proportion of Community") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = order_colors) +
  xlab(NULL)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        title = element_text(size = 15))

20 Making a Genus taxa plot

# Convert phyloseq object into a data frame with relative abundance counts
genusabundance <- justbacteria %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Genus") %>%                     # agglomerate at phylum level
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x/sum(x)} ) %>% # Transform to rel. abundance
  psmelt() %>%                                         # Melt to long format
  arrange(Genus) 
head(genusabundance)
##                                                                                                                                                             OTU
## 1 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGGGTGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTAAAATGTGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCGGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTGTAACTGACGCTGAGGCACGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 2 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGGGTGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTAAAATGTGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCGGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTGTAACTGACGCTGAGGCACGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 3 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGGGTGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTAAAATGTGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCGGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTGTAACTGACGCTGAGGCACGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 4 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGGGTGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTAAAATGTGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCGGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTGTAACTGACGCTGAGGCACGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 5 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGGGTGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTAAAATGTGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCGGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTGTAACTGACGCTGAGGCACGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
## 6 TACGTAGGTGGCGAGCGTTATCCGGAATCATTGGGCGTAAAGGGTGCGTANNNNNNNNNNAGCGGTAAAATGTGTAGATATATGGAGGAACACCGGTGGCGAAGGCGGCTTGCTGGGCTGTAACTGACGCTGAGGCACGAAAGCGTGGGGAGCAAAT
##   Sample   Abundance SampleID BarcodeSequence  LinkerPrimerSequence
## 1     59 0.009708738       59    AGCTAAATGGAT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA
## 2      7 0.000000000        7    TACGAGCCCTAA GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA
## 3     44 0.000000000       44    CAGCCGCATATC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA
## 4     68 0.000000000       68    CTGTCCCGCAGT GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA
## 5     71 0.000000000       71    CATGGGTGTTAC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA
## 6      1 0.000000000        1    TCAGCCTCAGCC GTGTGYCAGCMGCCGCGGTAA
##   BarcodePlate Well Description Time                 Method Add_num
## 1           14  E11          59  48h               DDR-NaOH       2
## 2           14   A7           7  12h Wet-milling-corn-fiber       1
## 3           14   D8          44  24h        DDR-NaHCO3-ball       2
## 4           14   F8          68  48h        DDR-NaHCO3-ball       2
## 5           14  F11          71  48h                 Inulin       2
## 6           14   A1           1  12h                  blank       1
##                     sample                method_time  Kingdom     Phylum
## 1               DDR-NaOH-2               DDR-NaOH-48h Bacteria Firmicutes
## 2 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-1 Wet-milling-corn-fiber-12h Bacteria Firmicutes
## 3        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-2        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-24h Bacteria Firmicutes
## 4        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-2        DDR-NaHCO3-ball-48h Bacteria Firmicutes
## 5                 Inulin-2                 Inulin-48h Bacteria Firmicutes
## 6                  blank-1                  blank-12h Bacteria Firmicutes
##     Class              Order              Family                         Genus
## 1 Bacilli Erysipelotrichales Erysipelotrichaceae [Anaerorhabdus] furcosa group
## 2 Bacilli Erysipelotrichales Erysipelotrichaceae [Anaerorhabdus] furcosa group
## 3 Bacilli Erysipelotrichales Erysipelotrichaceae [Anaerorhabdus] furcosa group
## 4 Bacilli Erysipelotrichales Erysipelotrichaceae [Anaerorhabdus] furcosa group
## 5 Bacilli Erysipelotrichales Erysipelotrichaceae [Anaerorhabdus] furcosa group
## 6 Bacilli Erysipelotrichales Erysipelotrichaceae [Anaerorhabdus] furcosa group
unique(genusabundance$Genus)
##  [1] "[Anaerorhabdus] furcosa group"     "[Bacteroides] pectinophilus group"
##  [3] "[Ruminococcus] torques group"      "Acidaminococcus"                  
##  [5] "Anaerostignum"                     "Anaerovibrio"                     
##  [7] "Bilophila"                         "Butyrivibrio"                     
##  [9] "Christensenellaceae R-7 group"     "Cloacibacillus"                   
## [11] "Clostridium sensu stricto 1"       "Clostridium sensu stricto 13"     
## [13] "Coprococcus"                       "Desulfovibrio"                    
## [15] "dgA-11 gut group"                  "Dorea"                            
## [17] "Erysipelotrichaceae UCG-002"       "Faecalibacterium"                 
## [19] "Family XIII AD3011 group"          "Fusobacterium"                    
## [21] "Lactobacillus"                     "Megasphaera"                      
## [23] "Mitsuokella"                       "NA"                               
## [25] "NK4A214 group"                     "Oscillospira"                     
## [27] "Pandoraea"                         "Parabacteroides"                  
## [29] "Phascolarctobacterium"             "Prevotella"                       
## [31] "Prevotella_7"                      "Prevotella_9"                     
## [33] "Prevotellaceae NK3B31 group"       "Prevotellaceae UCG-001"           
## [35] "Prevotellaceae UCG-003"            "Prevotellaceae UCG-004"           
## [37] "Rikenellaceae RC9 gut group"       "Ruminococcus"                     
## [39] "Sarcina"                           "Sharpea"                          
## [41] "Streptococcus"                     "Succinivibrio"                    
## [43] "Treponema"                         "UCG-002"                          
## [45] "UCG-005"                           "UCG-009"
# Summarize the most abundant taxa, filter to only include taxa with > 1% abundance
all <- genusabundance %>%
  select(Genus, Method, Abundance, Time) %>%
  group_by(Genus, Method, Time) %>%
  summarize(
    avg_abundance = mean(Abundance)
  ) %>%
  filter(avg_abundance > 0.1)
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Genus', 'Method'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
all
## # A tibble: 63 x 4
## # Groups:   Genus, Method [27]
##    Genus                       Method     Time  avg_abundance
##    <chr>                       <chr>      <chr>         <dbl>
##  1 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 blank      12h           0.278
##  2 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 blank      24h           0.417
##  3 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 blank      48h           0.159
##  4 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 blank      72h           0.107
##  5 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 Corn-fiber 12h           0.139
##  6 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 Corn-fiber 24h           0.180
##  7 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 Corn-fiber 48h           0.175
##  8 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 Corn-fiber 72h           0.248
##  9 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 DDR-NaHCO3 12h           0.447
## 10 Clostridium sensu stricto 1 DDR-NaHCO3 24h           0.150
## # … with 53 more rows
genus_colors <- c(
  "#CBD588", "#5F7FC7", "orange","#DA5724", "#508578", "#CD9BCD",
  "#AD6F3B", "#673770","#D14285", "#652926", "#C84248", 
  "#8569D5", "#5E738F","#D1A33D", "#8A7C64", "#599861"
)

ggplot(all)+
  geom_col(mapping = aes(x = Method, y = avg_abundance, fill = Genus), position = "fill", show.legend = TRUE)+
  ylab("Proportion of Community") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genus_colors) +
  xlab(NULL)+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.y.left = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),
        title = element_text(size = 15))
